title: Which site first?
description: Score every site, and let the programme rank them honestly.

steps.title: The priority list
steps.description: Every site, ranked by its score.
steps.voiceScript: With hundreds of sites and a finite budget, this ranks them.
steps.captionText: With hundreds of sites and a finite budget, this ranks them.
steps.fallbackMessage: The priority list: not visible on this screen yet.

steps.title: Unscored is a QUESTION, not a zero
steps.description: An unscored site is not a bad site.
steps.voiceScript: A site nobody has scored yet is shown as a question, never as a zero. A zero would quietly send it to the bottom of the list and it would never be built.
steps.captionText: A site nobody has scored yet is shown as a question, never as a zero. A zero would quietly send it to the bottom of the list and it would never be built.
steps.fallbackMessage: Unscored is a QUESTION, not a zero: not visible on this screen yet.

steps.title: Score a site
steps.description: The scorecard: access, roof, load, risk.
steps.fallbackMessage: Score a site: the next screen is not reachable from here.

steps.title: 100 means NO risk
steps.description: Read the direction of the scale carefully.
steps.voiceScript: Mind the direction of the risk scale. One hundred means no risk, not maximum risk.
steps.captionText: Mind the direction of the risk scale. One hundred means no risk, not maximum risk.
steps.fallbackMessage: 100 means NO risk: not visible on this screen yet.

steps.title: Scoring is not deciding
steps.description: The scorer proposes. Another person decides.
steps.voiceScript: Scoring a site does not qualify it. Somebody else decides, on the evidence of the score. In a one-person organisation that separation relaxes -- and the audit record says so.
steps.captionText: Scoring a site does not qualify it. Somebody else decides, on the evidence of the score. In a one-person organisation that separation relaxes -- and the audit record says so.
steps.fallbackMessage: Scoring is not deciding: not visible on this screen yet.
